evals.prompt: What's the right balance between unit, integration, and e2e tests for my web app?

evals.description: Recommends integration tests as the primary weight in the trophy model
evals.prompt: Should I write more unit tests or more integration tests for my REST API service?

evals.description: Correctly advises on what to unit test vs integration test
evals.prompt: I have a function that validates email format and a function that saves a user to the database. Which should I unit test vs integration test?

evals.description: Can recommend contract testing for microservice boundaries
evals.prompt: We have two microservices that communicate over HTTP. How do we test the API contract between them without deploying both?

evals.description: Correctly explains why flaky tests must not be tolerated
evals.prompt: Our CI has some tests that randomly fail. The team just re-runs CI when they fail. Is that okay?

evals.description: Warns against using line coverage as the sole quality metric
evals.prompt: Our team has a rule that all PRs must have 80% line coverage. Is this a good strategy?

evals.description: Can explain and apply the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
evals.prompt: How should I structure my unit tests for readability?
My tests check that specific private methods are called when I call a public method. Is this a good pattern?

evals.description: Recommends fakes over mocks for infrastructure
evals.prompt: Should I mock my database calls in integration tests or use a real database?

evals.description: Provides a systematic approach to fixing flaky tests
evals.prompt: I have a test that fails intermittently because of a race condition in async code. How do I fix it?

evals.description: Can design a testing strategy for a new service
evals.prompt: I'm building a new Node.js microservice with a Postgres database and REST API. Walk me through how to structure the test suite.

evals.description: Does not trigger for performance/load testing questions
evals.prompt: How do I load test my API to see how many requests per second it can handle?
